Genes.Biological_Significance: Co-expression with Ros1 achieved anthocyanin levels comparable to blackberries and blueberries; Del/Ros1 activated a broader spectrum of phenylpropanoid/flavonoid genes than maize Lc/C1 (including PAL and F3'5'H); dietary supplementation with purple tomato powder significantly extended lifespan of Trp53-/- cancer-susceptible mice (182.2 ± 8.6 d vs 142.0 ± 8.7 d on standard diet, P < 0.05)
Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Massive increase in anthocyanins (up to 2.83 ± 0.46 mg/g FW in Del/Ros1N line, from virtually undetectable in wild-type); increased flavonols in peel
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: Co-expressed with Ros1 under fruit-specific E8 promoter in transgenic tomato; four independent transgenic lines (C, N, Y, Z) generated; anthocyanin accumulation confirmed by spectrophotometry and HPLC-ESI-MS/MS; northern blot and microarray confirmed upregulation of nearly all anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (PAL, CHS, CHI, F3H, F3'H, F3'5'H, DFR, ANS, 3-GT, RT, AAC, 5-GT, GST, PAT); PAL enzyme activity increased 200-fold in Del/Ros1N; CHI activity increased 3-fold; hydrophilic antioxidant capacity (TEAC) increased 3-fold; suppression subtractive hybridization identified differentially expressed genes
Genes.Target_Nutrient_Metabolites: Anthocyanins (delphinidin 3-(caffeoyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, delphinidin 3-(cis-coumaroyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, delphinidin 3-(trans-coumaroyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, delphinidin 3-(feruloyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, petunidin 3-(trans-coumaroyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, petunidin 3-(feruloyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside); also flavonols (rutin, myricetin-rhamnoglucoside, kaempferol-rhamnoglucoside)
Genes.Tissue_Specificity: Fruit-specific expression driven by E8 promoter; anthocyanin accumulation in peel, pericarp, and inner flesh; no abnormal anthocyanin accumulation in stems or leaves

Genes.Biological_Significance: Co-expression with Del achieved anthocyanin levels comparable to blackberries and blueberries; Ros1 (MYB) interacts with Del (bHLH) to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis; Del/Ros1 activated F3'5'H enabling production of dihydromyricetin (the preferred DFR substrate in Solanaceae), explaining success over maize Lc/C1 which failed to produce anthocyanins in tomato; dietary supplementation with purple tomato powder significantly extended lifespan of Trp53-/- cancer-susceptible mice (182.2 ± 8.6 d vs 142.0 ± 8.7 d, P < 0.05)
Genes.Effect_on_Metabolite: Massive increase in anthocyanins (up to 2.83 ± 0.46 mg/g FW in Del/Ros1N line, from virtually undetectable in wild-type); increased flavonols in peel
Genes.Experimental_Evidence: Co-expressed with Del under fruit-specific E8 promoter in transgenic tomato; four independent transgenic lines (C, N, Y, Z) generated; anthocyanin accumulation confirmed by spectrophotometry and HPLC-ESI-MS/MS; northern blot and microarray confirmed upregulation of nearly all anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (PAL, CHS, CHI, F3H, F3'H, F3'5'H, DFR, ANS, 3-GT, RT, AAC, 5-GT, GST, PAT); PAL enzyme activity increased 200-fold in Del/Ros1N; CHI activity increased 3-fold; hydrophilic antioxidant capacity (TEAC) increased 3-fold; interaction with Del (bHLH) is required for full activation — co-expression of both MYB and bHLH gives much higher gene induction than either alone
Genes.Target_Nutrient_Metabolites: Anthocyanins (delphinidin 3-(caffeoyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, delphinidin 3-(cis-coumaroyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, delphinidin 3-(trans-coumaroyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, delphinidin 3-(feruloyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, petunidin 3-(trans-coumaroyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside, petunidin 3-(feruloyl)-rutinoside-5-glucoside); also flavonols (rutin, myricetin-rhamnoglucoside, kaempferol-rhamnoglucoside)
Genes.Tissue_Specificity: Fruit-specific expression driven by E8 promoter; anthocyanin accumulation in peel, pericarp, and inner flesh; no abnormal anthocyanin accumulation in stems or leaves

Title: Enrichment of tomato fruit with health-promoting anthocyanins by expression of select transcription factors
